summary: Alpha-fetoprotein has robust scientific backing as the most reliable biomarker for detecting neural tube defects due to its consistent association with these conditions across multiple research sources. Its use enhances accuracy when combined with additional screening methods like ultrasound or genetic testing during pregnancy. To directly answer your query about which test serves optimally here, I would recommend focusing primarily on monitoring AFP concentrations within maternal blood samples throughout gestation periods where risk factors are present. This approach aligns closely with current medical guidelines emphasizing early detection through comprehensive assessments including but not limited to serum-based tests measuring specific proteins
